message:
fix(core): preserve sqlite_master rowid ordering in ALTER TABLE ADD/DROP COLUMN (#17)

ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN (and DROP COLUMN, RENAME COLUMN) previously used
delete_sqlite_master_row + insert_sqlite_master_row, which assigned a new
(higher) rowid to the table entry. Standard SQLite reads sqlite_master in
rowid order and expects table entries to appear before their index entries.
When a table had indexes, the re-inserted table entry would get a rowid
higher than the index entries, causing "malformed database schema" errors.

Fix: add update_sqlite_master_sql() that finds the existing row, captures
its original rowid, deletes it, and re-inserts with the same rowid — an
in-place update that preserves rowid ordering. RENAME TO still uses the
old delete+insert path since it changes the name/tbl_name columns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
